instruction: Build a Proxy-based reactivity system in JavaScript that supports deep reactive objects, computed properties with lazy evaluation, and batched DOM updates, similar to Vue 3's reactivity engine.
output:
<think>
Vue 3's reactivity engine is built on Proxy and uses a dependency tracking system. Let me design a simplified but complete version.

Core concepts:
1. reactive(): wraps objects in Proxy that tracks get/set
2. effect(): runs a function and auto-tracks which reactive properties it reads
3. computed(): lazy evaluated derived values that cache and invalidate
4. Batched updates: coalesce multiple changes into one DOM update using microtask queue

Dependency tracking:
- Global variable `activeEffect` points to currently executing effect
- On proxy get: record (target, key) -> activeEffect mapping
- On proxy set: look up all effects that depend on (target, key), schedule them

Data structures:
- targetMap: WeakMap<target, Map<key, Set<effect>>> — tracks dependencies
- effectQueue: Set<effect> — pending effects to run, deduplicated
- isFlushing: boolean — whether we've scheduled a microtask flush

Deep reactivity: when a get returns an object, wrap it in reactive() lazily. Cache the proxy to avoid creating duplicate proxies for the same object.

Computed properties:
- An effect that runs the getter and caches the value
- dirty flag: set to true when dependencies change
- On read: if dirty, recompute. Otherwise return cached value
- A computed is itself reactive — other effects can depend on it

Batched updates: instead of running effects synchronously on set, push them to a queue. Schedule a microtask (queueMicrotask or Promise.then) to flush the queue. This coalesces multiple synchronous changes into one update cycle.
</think>

```javascript
// ===== Core reactivity engine =====

const targetMap = new WeakMap();
const proxyCache = new WeakMap();
let activeEffect = null;
const effectStack = [];
const pendingEffects = new Set();
let isFlushing = false;

function track(target, key) {
  if (!activeEffect) return;
  let depsMap = targetMap.get(target);
  if (!depsMap) {
    depsMap = new Map();
    targetMap.set(target, depsMap);
  }
  let deps = depsMap.get(key);
  if (!deps) {
    deps = new Set();
    depsMap.set(key, deps);
  }
  deps.add(activeEffect);
  activeEffect.deps.add(deps);
}

function trigger(target, key) {
  const depsMap = targetMap.get(target);
  if (!depsMap) return;
  const deps = depsMap.get(key);
  if (!deps) return;
  // Schedule effects instead of running immediately
  deps.forEach((eff) => {
    if (eff !== activeEffect) {
      if (eff.scheduler) {
        eff.scheduler();
      } else {
        queueEffect(eff);
      }
    }
  });
}

function queueEffect(eff) {
  pendingEffects.add(eff);
  if (!isFlushing) {
    isFlushing = true;
    queueMicrotask(flushEffects);
  }
}

function flushEffects() {
  const effects = [...pendingEffects];
  pendingEffects.clear();
  isFlushing = false;
  effects.forEach((eff) => eff.run());
}

// ===== reactive() =====

function reactive(target) {
  if (typeof target !== 'object' || target === null) return target;
  if (proxyCache.has(target)) return proxyCache.get(target);

  const proxy = new Proxy(target, {
    get(obj, key, receiver) {
      if (key === '__raw') return obj;
      track(obj, key);
      const value = Reflect.get(obj, key, receiver);
      // Deep reactivity: wrap nested objects lazily
      if (typeof value === 'object' && value !== null) {
        return reactive(value);
      }
      return value;
    },
    set(obj, key, value, receiver) {
      const oldVal = obj[key];
      const result = Reflect.set(obj, key, value, receiver);
      if (oldVal !== value) {
        trigger(obj, key);
      }
      return result;
    },
    deleteProperty(obj, key) {
      const had = key in obj;
      const result = Reflect.deleteProperty(obj, key);
      if (had) trigger(obj, key);
      return result;
    },
  });

  proxyCache.set(target, proxy);
  return proxy;
}

// ===== effect() =====

function effect(fn, options = {}) {
  const eff = {
    run() {
      cleanup(eff);
      effectStack.push(activeEffect);
      activeEffect = eff;
      try {
        return fn();
      } finally {
        activeEffect = effectStack.pop();
      }
    },
    deps: new Set(),
    scheduler: options.scheduler || null,
  };
  if (!options.lazy) {
    eff.run();
  }
  return eff;
}

function cleanup(eff) {
  eff.deps.forEach((depSet) => depSet.delete(eff));
  eff.deps.clear();
}

// ===== computed() =====

function computed(getter) {
  let cached;
  let dirty = true;

  const eff = effect(getter, {
    lazy: true,
    scheduler() {
      if (!dirty) {
        dirty = true;
        // Trigger effects that depend on this computed
        trigger(obj, 'value');
      }
    },
  });

  const obj = {
    get value() {
      track(obj, 'value');
      if (dirty) {
        cached = eff.run();
        dirty = false;
      }
      return cached;
    },
  };

  return obj;
}

// ===== Demo: Simulated DOM updates =====

const state = reactive({
  user: { name: 'Alice', age: 30 },
  items: ['apple', 'banana'],
  count: 0,
});

const fullInfo = computed(() => {
  return `${state.user.name} (${state.user.age}) - ${state.items.length} items`;
});

// DOM update effect — batched
let renderCount = 0;
effect(() => {
  renderCount++;
  console.log(`[Render #${renderCount}] ${fullInfo.value}, count=${state.count}`);
});

// Multiple synchronous changes — only ONE batched render
console.log('\n--- Batched update ---');
state.count = 1;
state.count = 2;
state.count = 3;
state.user.name = 'Bob';
// All 4 changes batched into 1 render via microtask

// Deep reactivity
setTimeout(() => {
  console.log('\n--- Deep reactivity ---');
  state.user.age = 31;  // triggers re-render
  state.items.push('cherry');  // triggers re-render
}, 10);

setTimeout(() => {
  console.log(`\nTotal renders: ${renderCount}`);
}, 50);
```

instruction: Implement Symbol usage patterns in JavaScript for metaprogramming: Symbol.iterator, Symbol.toPrimitive, Symbol.hasInstance, Symbol.species, and custom symbols for private-like properties and protocol definition.
output:
<think>
Symbols are unique, immutable identifiers primarily used for metaprogramming — customizing how objects interact with language features.

Key well-known symbols to demonstrate:

1. Symbol.iterator — makes objects iterable (for..of, spread, destructuring)
2. Symbol.toPrimitive — customize type coercion (to number, string, etc.)
3. Symbol.hasInstance — customize instanceof checks
4. Symbol.species — control what constructor derived types use for new instances
5. Custom symbols for "private" properties and protocol definition

For Symbol.iterator, I'll create a Range class that's iterable.

For Symbol.toPrimitive, I'll create a Money class that converts differently for string vs number context.

For Symbol.hasInstance, I'll create a type checker that works with instanceof on plain objects.

For Symbol.species, I'll show a subclass of Array where filter/map return the base Array instead of the subclass.

For custom symbols as protocols: define a symbol like `Symbol.for('serialize')` and use it as a protocol that any class can implement, similar to how Rust traits or Go interfaces work but opt-in.

I should also explain Symbol.for() vs Symbol() — Symbol.for creates/retrieves global symbols (shared across realms), while Symbol() creates unique local symbols.
</think>

```javascript
// ===== Symbol.iterator: Custom iterable =====

class Range {
  constructor(start, end, step = 1) {
    this.start = start;
    this.end = end;
    this.step = step;
  }

  [Symbol.iterator]() {
    let current = this.start;
    const { end, step } = this;
    return {
      next() {
        if (current <= end) {
          const value = current;
          current += step;
          return { value, done: false };
        }
        return { done: true };
      },
    };
  }
}

const range = new Range(1, 5);
console.log('Range:', [...range]); // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
const [a, b, ...rest] = new Range(10, 15);
console.log('Destructured:', a, b, rest); // 10 11 [12, 13, 14, 15]

// ===== Symbol.toPrimitive: Custom type coercion =====

class Money {
  constructor(amount, currency) {
    this.amount = amount;
    this.currency = currency;
  }

  [Symbol.toPrimitive](hint) {
    switch (hint) {
      case 'number':  return this.amount;
      case 'string':  return `${this.currency}${this.amount.toFixed(2)}`;
      case 'default': return this.amount; // used by == and +
    }
  }
}

const price = new Money(19.99, '$');
console.log(`\nPrice: ${price}`);     // "$19.99" (string hint)
console.log('Tax:', price * 0.08);    // 1.5992 (number hint)
console.log('Total:', price + 5);     // 24.99 (default hint)

// ===== Symbol.hasInstance: Custom instanceof =====

class TypeChecker {
  constructor(validatorFn) {
    this.validate = validatorFn;
  }

  [Symbol.hasInstance](value) {
    return this.validate(value);
  }
}

const isPositiveNumber = new TypeChecker(
  (v) => typeof v === 'number' && v > 0 && Number.isFinite(v)
);

const isNonEmptyString = new TypeChecker(
  (v) => typeof v === 'string' && v.length > 0
);

console.log('\n42 instanceof isPositiveNumber:', 42 instanceof isPositiveNumber);       // true
console.log('-1 instanceof isPositiveNumber:', -1 instanceof isPositiveNumber);        // false
console.log('"hi" instanceof isNonEmptyString:', 'hi' instanceof isNonEmptyString);    // true

// ===== Symbol.species: Control constructor for derived instances =====

class SafeArray extends Array {
  // Return plain Array from filter/map/etc instead of SafeArray
  static get [Symbol.species]() {
    return Array;
  }

  sum() {
    return this.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
  }
}

const sa = SafeArray.from([1, 2, 3, 4, 5]);
console.log('\nSafeArray sum:', sa.sum()); // 15
const filtered = sa.filter((x) => x > 2);
console.log('Filtered type:', filtered.constructor.name); // "Array", not "SafeArray"
// console.log(filtered.sum()); // TypeError — it's a plain Array

// ===== Custom symbols as protocols =====

// Define a serialization protocol
const Serializable = Symbol.for('protocol.Serializable');
const Deserializable = Symbol.for('protocol.Deserializable');

class User {
  constructor(name, email) {
    this.name = name;
    this.email = email;
  }

  [Serializable]() {
    return JSON.stringify({ type: 'User', name: this.name, email: this.email });
  }

  static [Deserializable](data) {
    const obj = JSON.parse(data);
    return new User(obj.name, obj.email);
  }
}

class Product {
  constructor(name, price) {
    this.name = name;
    this.price = price;
  }

  [Serializable]() {
    return JSON.stringify({ type: 'Product', name: this.name, price: this.price });
  }
}

// Generic function that works with any Serializable
function serialize(obj) {
  if (typeof obj[Serializable] !== 'function') {
    throw new Error(`Object does not implement Serializable protocol`);
  }
  return obj[Serializable]();
}

const user = new User('Alice', 'alice@example.com');
const product = new Product('Widget', 29.99);

console.log('\nSerialized user:', serialize(user));
console.log('Serialized product:', serialize(product));

// Deserialize
const restored = User[Deserializable](serialize(user));
console.log('Restored:', restored);

// ===== Private-like symbols =====

const _internal = Symbol('internal');
const _validate = Symbol('validate');

class Config {
  constructor(data) {
    this[_internal] = { ...data }; // not enumerable via Object.keys
    this[_validate]();
  }

  [_validate]() {
    if (!this[_internal].host) throw new Error('host required');
  }

  get host() { return this[_internal].host; }
  get port() { return this[_internal].port || 8080; }
}

const cfg = new Config({ host: 'localhost', port: 3000 });
console.log('\nConfig host:', cfg.host);
console.log('Visible keys:', Object.keys(cfg)); // [] — symbols not enumerable
console.log('Has _internal?', Reflect.ownKeys(cfg).length > 0); // true via Reflect
```

instruction: Implement WeakRef and FinalizationRegistry patterns in JavaScript for building a resource-safe cache system that automatically evicts entries when memory pressure increases, with observable cleanup callbacks.
output:
<think>
WeakRef and FinalizationRegistry (ES2021) enable patterns that were previously impossible in JavaScript:

1. WeakRef: holds a weak reference to an object. The object can be GC'd even though the WeakRef exists. Calling .deref() returns the object or undefined.

2. FinalizationRegistry: registers a callback that fires when a registered object is garbage collected. It receives a "held value" (not the object itself, since it's already gone).

Use case: a cache that holds values weakly. When memory pressure causes GC to collect cached objects, the cache automatically cleans up the entry. The FinalizationRegistry tells us which cache key was evicted.

Design:
- WeakValueCache<K, V>: Map<K, WeakRef<V>> with FinalizationRegistry
- On get: deref the WeakRef. If undefined, the value was GC'd — remove the entry.
- On set: create WeakRef, register with FinalizationRegistry
- Cleanup callback: remove the key from the map

Important caveats:
- FinalizationRegistry callbacks are non-deterministic (may fire later or not at all)
- WeakRef.deref() should be checked every time — don't cache the result across turns
- Values must be objects (primitives can't be weakly held)
- Don't rely on finalization for correctness — only for optimization/cleanup

I'll also show a ResourceManager pattern where resources (file handles, connections) are automatically closed when the owning object is GC'd — as a safety net, not a replacement for explicit cleanup.
</think>

```javascript
// ===== WeakValue Cache with automatic eviction =====

class WeakCache {
  #entries = new Map();  // key -> WeakRef<value>
  #registry;
  #evictionLog = [];
  #stats = { hits: 0, misses: 0, evictions: 0 };

  constructor(options = {}) {
    const { onEvict } = options;

    this.#registry = new FinalizationRegistry((key) => {
      // Called when a cached value is garbage collected
      this.#entries.delete(key);
      this.#stats.evictions++;
      this.#evictionLog.push({ key, time: Date.now() });
      if (onEvict) onEvict(key);
    });
  }

  set(key, value) {
    if (typeof value !== 'object' || value === null) {
      throw new TypeError('WeakCache values must be objects');
    }

    // Unregister old value if exists
    const existing = this.#entries.get(key);
    if (existing) {
      const old = existing.deref();
      if (old) this.#registry.unregister(old);
    }

    const ref = new WeakRef(value);
    this.#entries.set(key, ref);
    this.#registry.register(value, key, value); // (target, heldValue, unregisterToken)
  }

  get(key) {
    const ref = this.#entries.get(key);
    if (!ref) {
      this.#stats.misses++;
      return undefined;
    }

    const value = ref.deref();
    if (value === undefined) {
      // Value was GC'd but finalization hasn't run yet
      this.#entries.delete(key);
      this.#stats.misses++;
      return undefined;
    }

    this.#stats.hits++;
    return value;
  }

  has(key) {
    const ref = this.#entries.get(key);
    return ref ? ref.deref() !== undefined : false;
  }

  delete(key) {
    const ref = this.#entries.get(key);
    if (ref) {
      const value = ref.deref();
      if (value) this.#registry.unregister(value);
      this.#entries.delete(key);
    }
  }

  get size() {
    // Count only live entries
    let count = 0;
    for (const [key, ref] of this.#entries) {
      if (ref.deref() !== undefined) count++;
      else this.#entries.delete(key); // cleanup stale
    }
    return count;
  }

  get stats() { return { ...this.#stats }; }
  get evictionLog() { return [...this.#evictionLog]; }
}

// ===== Resource Manager with safety-net cleanup =====

class ResourceManager {
  #resources = new Map();  // id -> WeakRef<resource>
  #registry;
  #cleanupFns = new Map(); // id -> cleanup function

  constructor() {
    this.#registry = new FinalizationRegistry((id) => {
      console.warn(`[ResourceManager] Resource ${id} was GC'd without explicit cleanup!`);
      const cleanup = this.#cleanupFns.get(id);
      if (cleanup) {
        try { cleanup(); } catch (e) { console.error(`Cleanup failed for ${id}:`, e); }
      }
      this.#resources.delete(id);
      this.#cleanupFns.delete(id);
    });
  }

  register(id, resource, cleanupFn) {
    this.#resources.set(id, new WeakRef(resource));
    this.#cleanupFns.set(id, cleanupFn);
    this.#registry.register(resource, id, resource);
  }

  // Explicit cleanup — preferred path
  release(id) {
    const ref = this.#resources.get(id);
    if (ref) {
      const resource = ref.deref();
      if (resource) this.#registry.unregister(resource);
      const cleanup = this.#cleanupFns.get(id);
      if (cleanup) cleanup();
    }
    this.#resources.delete(id);
    this.#cleanupFns.delete(id);
  }
}

// ===== Demo =====

const cache = new WeakCache({
  onEvict: (key) => console.log(`  [evicted] ${key}`),
});

// Store objects in cache
let userData = { name: 'Alice', data: new Array(10000).fill('x') };
let configData = { settings: { theme: 'dark' } };

cache.set('user', userData);
cache.set('config', configData);

console.log('Cache size:', cache.size);     // 2
console.log('Get user:', cache.get('user')?.name); // 'Alice'

// Drop strong reference — value becomes eligible for GC
userData = null;

// Force GC (only works with --expose-gc flag in Node.js)
if (typeof globalThis.gc === 'function') {
  globalThis.gc();
  // After GC, the 'user' entry will be evicted
  setTimeout(() => {
    console.log('\nAfter GC:');
    console.log('Cache size:', cache.size);
    console.log('Get user:', cache.get('user'));  // undefined
    console.log('Stats:', cache.stats);
  }, 100);
}

// Resource manager demo
const rm = new ResourceManager();

let conn = { socket: 'tcp://localhost:5432', status: 'open' };
rm.register('db-conn', conn, () => {
  console.log('  [cleanup] Closing database connection');
});

// Preferred: explicit release
rm.release('db-conn');
// Output: [cleanup] Closing database connection

console.log('\nWeakRef/FinalizationRegistry patterns demonstrated.');
console.log('Note: GC behavior is non-deterministic. Run with --expose-gc for demo.');
```

instruction: Implement SharedArrayBuffer and Atomics patterns in JavaScript for building a lock-free concurrent data structure shared between Web Workers, including a ring buffer and a spin lock.
output:
<think>
SharedArrayBuffer enables true shared memory between JavaScript threads (Web Workers). Atomics provides atomic operations for synchronization without data races.

Key concepts:
1. SharedArrayBuffer: allocates memory visible to multiple workers
2. Atomics.load/store: atomic read/write (prevent tearing)
3. Atomics.compareExchange (CAS): lock-free primitive
4. Atomics.wait/notify: blocking wait and wake (only in workers, not main thread)
5. Atomics.add: atomic increment

I'll implement:
1. A spin lock using Atomics.compareExchange — simple mutual exclusion
2. A lock-free SPSC (single-producer, single-consumer) ring buffer
3. A shared counter for coordination

The SPSC ring buffer:
- Uses a SharedArrayBuffer for the data
- Two atomic indices: head (write position) and tail (read position)
- Producer writes at head, advances head
- Consumer reads at tail, advances tail
- Full when (head + 1) % size == tail
- Empty when head == tail
- Lock-free because only one thread modifies each index

For the spin lock:
- A single Int32 in shared memory: 0 = unlocked, 1 = locked
- Lock: CAS(0, 1) in a loop
- Unlock: Atomics.store(0)

Since we can't actually create Workers in a single code snippet, I'll structure the code as a shared module that both main thread and workers would import.
</think>

```javascript
// ===== Shared memory utilities for Web Workers =====

// --- Spin Lock using Atomics ---

class SpinLock {
  /**
   * @param {SharedArrayBuffer} sab - Must have at least 4 bytes
   * @param {number} offset - Byte offset for the lock int32
   */
  constructor(sab, offset = 0) {
    this.view = new Int32Array(sab, offset, 1);
  }

  lock() {
    // Spin until we successfully CAS 0 -> 1
    while (Atomics.compareExchange(this.view, 0, 0, 1) !== 0) {
      // Busy wait — for short critical sections only
    }
  }

  unlock() {
    Atomics.store(this.view, 0, 0);
  }

  tryLock() {
    return Atomics.compareExchange(this.view, 0, 0, 1) === 0;
  }

  withLock(fn) {
    this.lock();
    try {
      return fn();
    } finally {
      this.unlock();
    }
  }
}

// --- Lock-free SPSC Ring Buffer ---

class SharedRingBuffer {
  /**
   * Layout in SharedArrayBuffer:
   *   [0]: head index (producer writes here)
   *   [1]: tail index (consumer reads here)
   *   [2..capacity+2]: data slots
   */
  constructor(capacity) {
    // +2 for head and tail indices, +1 because ring buffer wastes one slot
    const totalSlots = capacity + 1;
    const totalInts = totalSlots + 2; // 2 for head, tail
    this.sab = new SharedArrayBuffer(totalInts * 4);
    this.meta = new Int32Array(this.sab, 0, 2);     // [head, tail]
    this.data = new Int32Array(this.sab, 8, totalSlots);
    this.capacity = totalSlots;
  }

  // For passing to workers
  getBufferInfo() {
    return { sab: this.sab, capacity: this.capacity };
  }

  // Reconstruct from buffer info (in worker)
  static fromBufferInfo({ sab, capacity }) {
    const rb = Object.create(SharedRingBuffer.prototype);
    rb.sab = sab;
    rb.meta = new Int32Array(sab, 0, 2);
    rb.data = new Int32Array(sab, 8, capacity);
    rb.capacity = capacity;
    return rb;
  }

  // Producer: write value, return true if successful
  push(value) {
    const head = Atomics.load(this.meta, 0);
    const tail = Atomics.load(this.meta, 1);
    const nextHead = (head + 1) % this.capacity;

    if (nextHead === tail) {
      return false; // full
    }

    this.data[head] = value;
    // Store with release semantics — ensure data write is visible before head update
    Atomics.store(this.meta, 0, nextHead);
    return true;
  }

  // Consumer: read value, return { value, ok }
  pop() {
    const head = Atomics.load(this.meta, 0);
    const tail = Atomics.load(this.meta, 1);

    if (head === tail) {
      return { value: 0, ok: false }; // empty
    }

    const value = this.data[tail];
    const nextTail = (tail + 1) % this.capacity;
    Atomics.store(this.meta, 1, nextTail);
    return { value, ok: true };
  }

  get size() {
    const head = Atomics.load(this.meta, 0);
    const tail = Atomics.load(this.meta, 1);
    return (head - tail + this.capacity) % this.capacity;
  }

  get isEmpty() { return this.size === 0; }
  get isFull() { return this.size === this.capacity - 1; }
}

// --- Atomic Counter ---

class SharedCounter {
  constructor(sab, offset = 0) {
    this.view = new Int32Array(sab, offset, 1);
  }

  increment() { return Atomics.add(this.view, 0, 1) + 1; }
  decrement() { return Atomics.sub(this.view, 0, 1) - 1; }
  get value() { return Atomics.load(this.view, 0); }
  set value(v) { Atomics.store(this.view, 0, v); }
}

// --- Wait/Notify Barrier ---

class SharedBarrier {
  constructor(numThreads) {
    this.sab = new SharedArrayBuffer(8);
    this.count = new Int32Array(this.sab, 0, 1);  // arrived count
    this.phase = new Int32Array(this.sab, 4, 1);  // phase counter
    this.numThreads = numThreads;
  }

  // Called by each worker thread
  async arrive() {
    const currentPhase = Atomics.load(this.phase, 0);
    const arrived = Atomics.add(this.count, 0, 1) + 1;

    if (arrived === this.numThreads) {
      // Last to arrive — reset and advance phase
      Atomics.store(this.count, 0, 0);
      Atomics.add(this.phase, 0, 1);
      Atomics.notify(this.phase, 0); // wake all waiters
    } else {
      // Wait for phase to advance
      // Note: Atomics.wait only works in workers, not main thread
      Atomics.wait(this.phase, 0, currentPhase);
    }
  }
}

// ===== Demo (single-threaded simulation) =====

// Ring buffer
const rb = new SharedRingBuffer(8);
console.log('--- Ring Buffer ---');
for (let i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
  console.log(`push(${i}):`, rb.push(i));
}
console.log('Size:', rb.size);

while (!rb.isEmpty) {
  const { value, ok } = rb.pop();
  if (ok) console.log(`pop: ${value}`);
}
console.log('Empty:', rb.isEmpty);

// Spin lock
const lockBuf = new SharedArrayBuffer(4);
const lock = new SpinLock(lockBuf);
let sharedValue = 0;

lock.withLock(() => {
  sharedValue += 10;
  console.log('\n--- Spin Lock ---');
  console.log('Protected update:', sharedValue);
});

// Counter
const counterBuf = new SharedArrayBuffer(4);
const counter = new SharedCounter(counterBuf);
console.log('\n--- Atomic Counter ---');
console.log('Increment:', counter.increment()); // 1
console.log('Increment:', counter.increment()); // 2
console.log('Value:', counter.value);            // 2
```
